intro.uz: Thought Mastery — fikrlaringizni boshqarish va yoʻnaltirish. AJ Hoge NLP (neuro-linguistic programming) yordamida miyani xuddi kompyuterdek dasturlashni, salbiy tasavvurlarni ijobiyga almashtirishni koʻrsatadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi shu ichki suhbat tilida: gerundiy va infinitiv 1 (enjoy doing / want to do) va hozirgi zamon xulosasi (must / might / can't be).
intro.en: Thought Mastery — controlling and directing your own thoughts. AJ Hoge uses NLP (neuro-linguistic programming) to show how to program your brain like a computer and swap negative pictures for positive ones. This week's two grammar topics are the language of that inner talk: gerund vs infinitive 1 (enjoy doing / want to do) and modals of present deduction (must / might / can't be).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida enjoy/keep/stop dan keyin -ing, want/decide/need dan keyin to + feʼlni toʻxtamasdan toʻgʻri ishlata olasiz va biror fikr yoki holat haqida ishonch darajangizni (must be / might be / can't be) ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can correctly use -ing after enjoy/keep/stop and to + verb after want/decide/need without pausing, and say how sure you are about a thought or situation (must be / might be / can't be).

grammar.titleUz: Gerundiy va infinitiv 1: enjoy doing / want to do
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — say what you enjoy doing and what you decided to do
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can choose -ing or to + verb correctly after common verbs
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida bu farq yoʻq: “oʻqishni yaxshi koʻraman” ham, “oʻqishni xohlayman” ham bir xil “-ish” bilan yasaladi. Ingliz tili esa ikkiga ajratadi: enjoy/keep/stop dan keyin feʼl + -ing (I enjoy studying), lekin want/decide/need dan keyin to + feʼl (I want to study).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I want studying. I enjoy to learn. ✅ I want to study. I enjoy learning. want/decide/need/hope → to + feʼl; enjoy/keep/stop/finish/avoid → feʼl + -ing. Buni feʼllar guruhi bilan eslab qoling.

examples.en: She kept listening to Swahili every day.
examples.uz: U har kuni Swahilini tinglashda davom etdi.

examples.en: I decided to change my morning routine.

exercises.prompt: I enjoy ___ to English every morning.
She decided ___ only 30 minutes a day.
exercises.hintUz: decide dan keyin to + feʼl: to sleep

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “I enjoy … . I want to … . I decided to … .” Har uchala qolipni ishlating.

grammar.titleUz: Hozirgi zamon xulosasi: must / might / can't be
grammar.titleEn: Modals of present deduction: must / might / can't be
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking — sound natural when you guess and deduce
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can express how certain I am (must / might / can't be)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha taxminni “-sa kerak”, “boʻlishi mumkin”, “boʻlmasa kerak” bilan aytamiz: “Band boʻlsa kerak”. Ingliz tili buni bitta modal soʻzga jamlaydi: “He must be busy.” Oʻzbek oʻquvchi koʻpincha faqat “maybe he is busy” deydi — bu xato emas, ammo “he must be busy” ancha tabiiyroq va aniqroq eshitiladi.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ He must to be busy. He must is busy. ✅ He must be busy. must/might/could/can't + be (feʼlga to ham, -s ham qoʻshilmaydi). “Aniq yoʻq” uchun mustn't emas, can't be ishlatiladi: It can't be true.

examples.en: This computer is so slow — it must have a virus.
examples.uz: Bu kompyuter juda sekin — virus boʻlsa kerak.

examples.en: That negative thought might be just fear.
examples.uz: U salbiy fikr shunchaki qoʻrquv boʻlishi mumkin.

examples.en: That can't be true — I don't believe it.
examples.uz: Bu rost boʻlishi mumkin emas — ishonmayman.

exercises.prompt: He isn't answering his phone. He ___ busy.
I'm not sure — it ___ a small mistake.

exercises.promptUz: Atrofingizdagi bir narsa haqida ovoz chiqarib uchta taxmin ayting: “… must be … . … might be … . … can't be … .”
exercises.hintUz: modal + be; ishonchga qarab must / might / can't be tanlang

vocab.uz: miyani til va tasavvurlar orqali “dasturlash” usuli
vocab.defEn: a method for managing your brain using language and images
vocab.example: AJ explains thought mastery using neuro-linguistic programming.

vocab.defEn: to change something a little bit, not totally
vocab.example: You can modify the picture in your head.

vocab.defEn: to make something stronger, higher or greater
vocab.example: This will boost up your motivation to learn.

vocab.defEn: the situation of being excellent at something
vocab.example: You want total mastery of English.

vocab.uz: biror sohada mukammal darajaga ega boʻlmoq
vocab.defEn: to be at the very top level of a skill
to control and direct what you think
vocab.example: You must manage your thoughts and emotions.

vocab.defEn: to move your thinking from one thing to another
vocab.example: When you shift your thoughts, you shift your emotions.

vocab.defEn: to train your brain to react automatically, like a computer
vocab.example: You program your brain by repeating the process again and again.

vocab.defEn: a bad or unhappy picture you make in your head
vocab.example: Take a negative image and make it smaller and darker.

vocab.defEn: to make your desire to act stronger

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda kompyuter qayta-qayta qotib qoladi, Samuel esa unda koʻplab zararlangan fayllar borligini aniqlaydi. AJ bugun miyani xuddi kompyuterdek dasturlash haqida gapiradi — salbiy fikrlar ham “virus” kabi ishlashiga xalaqit beradi. Sizningcha, virusning sababi nima — ehtiyotsiz internetmi yoki eskirgan himoya dasturmi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue a computer keeps freezing, and Samuel finds it is full of infected files. Today AJ talks about programming your brain like a computer — negative thoughts work like a “virus” that clogs it. What do you think caused the virus — careless browsing, or out-of-date software?

keyVocab.defEn: to stop working properly and not respond
to stop working or behaving properly
to go upstairs or come very soon
to get or acquire something, often without meaning to
to look through or search the internet
having all the most recent information or updates

quiz.q: How long do we look at our mobile phones for in a day, on average?
quiz.qUz: Oʻrtacha bir kunda telefonimizga qancha vaqt qaraymiz?
options: around two and a half hours
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) around two and a half hours. Tadqiqotga koʻra, oʻrtacha kuniga 2.42 soat — bu taxminan ikki yarim soat. Catherine ham xuddi shu javobni tanlab, toʻgʻri topdi.

vocab.uz: diqqat muddati (bir narsaga qancha vaqt eʼtibor bera olish)
vocab.defEn: the length of time we can focus on something
things which take away our focus or attention
criticism, especially in the media or newspapers
suspicious; seeming not quite right or honest

vocab.uz: bir vaqtda bir necha ish qilmoq
vocab.defEn: to do several things at the same time

funEnglish.title: How to Reprogram Your Mind and Master Your Thoughts

speakingPrompt.en: Think of a habit or belief you want to change. What do you enjoy doing, and what have you decided to change? When a negative thought comes, how do you check it — must it be true, or might it just be fear?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: gerundiy/infinitiv (I enjoy … , I decided to … , I keep …) va hozirgi zamon xulosasi (that thought must/might/can't be …).
